LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS DONALD FISHER, JAMES SUTTON AND KEVIN HENEGHAN'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER - OFF CALENDAR. DUPLICATE ENTRY. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
SUPPLEMENTAL REQUEST FOR JUDICILA NOTICE BY DONALD G. FISHER FILED BY DEFENDANT FISHER, DONALD G SUTTON, JAMES R. HENEGHAN, KEVIN

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DONALD FISHER'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. - THE DECISIONS IN KETCHUM V. MOSES (2001) 24 CAL.4TH 1122, 1131; ROSENAUR V. SUPERIOR COURT (2001) 88 CAL.APP.4TH 260, 287 AND RAMONA UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT V. TSIKNAS (2005) 135 CAL.APP.4TH 510, 523 THROUGH 525 ESTABLISH FISHER'S RIGHT TO RECOVER ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS. - LODESTAR IS THE APPROPRIATE METHOD TO DETERMINE THE FEES TO BE AWARDED (KETCHUM AT 1139), MEANING A CALCULATION OF THE NUMBER OF HOURS REASONABLY WORKED MULTIPLIED BY THE REASONABLE HOURLY RATE. FLANNERY V. PRENTICE (2001) 26 CAL.4TH 572, 584. THERE IS NO LEGAL OR FACTUAL BASIS TO ALLOCATE THE FEES AND COSTS AMONG THE PREVAILING DEFENDANTS. (RAMONA UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT SUPRA AT 525) - RECOVERABLE FEES INCLUDE FEES INCURRED IN ENFORCING THE RIGHT TO MANDATORY FEES. (KETCHUM SUPRA AT 1133-34) - FEES AND COSTS AWARDED OF $148,044.25 TO ACCOUNT FOR POSSIBLE DUPLICATION AND INEFFICIENCY. THE COURT DENIED THE RENEWED REQUEST FOR DISCOVERY BY ATTORNEY MELBOSTAD. THE COURT GRANTED THE REQUEST TO STRIKE THE PURPORTED FISHER DECLARATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUN-13-2007 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Motion And Motion In Support Of Application For Attys Fees
Off Calendar Jun-13-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DONALD FISHER'S Motion For ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS.
APPEARANCES: PAUL H. MELBOSTAD, ESQUIRE (GOLDSTEIN, GELLMAN, MELBOSTAD, GIBSON & HARRIS, LLP, 415.673.5600) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF IN PRO PER AS VOTER ON BEHALF OF PUBLIC. JAMES R. SUTTON, ESQUIRE AND GABE CAMARILLO, ESQUIRE (THE SUTTON LAW FIRM, 415.732.7700) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT DONALD FISHER.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED.
- THE DECISIONS IN KETCHUM V. MOSES (2001) 24 CAL.4TH 1122, 1131; ROSENAUR V. SUPERIOR COURT (2001) 88 CAL.APP.4TH 260, 287 AND RAMONA UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT V. TSIKNAS (2005) 135 CAL.APP.4TH 510, 523 THROUGH 525 ESTABLISH FISHER'S RIGHT TO RECOVER ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS.
- LODESTAR IS THE APPROPRIATE METHOD TO DETERMINE THE FEES TO BE AWARDED (KETCHUM AT 1139), MEANING A CALCULATION OF THE NUMBER OF HOURS REASONABLY WORKED MULTIPLIED BY THE REASONABLE HOURLY RATE. FLANNERY V. PRENTICE (2001) 26 CAL.4TH 572, 584. THERE IS NO LEGAL OR FACTUAL BASIS TO ALLOCATE THE FEES AND COSTS AMONG THE PREVAILING DEFENDANTS. (RAMONA UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT SUPRA AT 525)
- RECOVERABLE FEES INCLUDE FEES INCURRED IN ENFORCING THE RIGHT TO MANDATORY FEES. (KETCHUM SUPRA AT 1133-34)
- FEES AND COSTS AWARDED OF $148,044.25 TO ACCOUNT FOR POSSIBLE DUPLICATION AND INEFFICIENCY. THE COURT DENIED THE RENEWED REQUEST FOR DISCOVERY BY ATTORNEY MELBOSTAD. THE COURT GRANTED THE REQUEST TO STRIKE THE PURPORTED FISHER DECLARATION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600 (302/PJM/KD)
